title: Autonomous Cargo Resupply Operations Meet Critical Milestone
description: Skyways completed an autonomous cargo resupply flight to a Naval vessel on the Patuxent River, MD in March 2019. The 18.5-ft wingspan drone carried a 20-lb payload 40 miles to a moving vessel, landed, and returned autonomously.

title: Skyways and the Pursuit of Fully Autonomous Drone Deliveries
author: Juan Plaza
description: CEO Charles Acknin discusses Skyways' founding in 2017, the hybrid fixed-wing design with 8 electric motors plus ICE engine, and how Skyways was selected out of 65 companies for the Navy's autonomous cargo program.

title: Update: US Navy acquires Skyways aircraft prototype for Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS
description: The US Navy acquired a Skyways logistics UAS prototype for the Blue Water Maritime Logistics program. Skyways demonstrated autonomous transport of a 9 kg payload to a moving ship 40 km away without refueling.

title: Navy Tests Autonomous Aerial Supply Drone From Its Newest Supercarrier
author: Joseph Trevithick
description: Skyways' V2.5 drone delivered cargo to the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) during the Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS demonstration. The aircraft features hybrid-electric design, AI-driven flight, 500-mile range, and 30-lb payload capacity.

title: Navy Considering Drone Delivery for Essential Parts At Sea
description: The Navy selected Skyways as a non-traditional partner for the Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS program. Skyways' V2.2 drone was tested on the USS Gerald R. Ford and demonstrated ship-to-ship capability from USS Bainbridge to USNS Joshua Humphreys.

title: Orbital UAV to support Skyways' unmanned cargo transportation program for US Navy
description: Skyways signed an MoU with Orbital Corporation (ASX: OEC) for a pre-production heavy fuel engine to be integrated into Skyways' next-generation UAS for the US Navy cargo transportation program.

title: Update: Skyways attempting autonomous folding wings for US Navy Blue Water UAS
description: Skyways is developing autonomously folding wings for its Group 3 UAS under the Navy Blue Water Maritime Logistics programme. The original $575,141 OTA grew to four times its original value. The aircraft uses dual-propulsion with electric VTOL and heavy-fuel gasoline engine.

title: US Navy completes drone delivery trials to and from ships
author: Bruce Crumley
description: Skyways and Martin UAV demonstrated VTOL cargo delivery over 200 nautical miles during Navy Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS trials. The program addresses the 90% of Navy logistics deliveries weighing under 50 lbs.

title: Navy Drone Delivery Trials Between Ships And Shore Successful
author: Haye Kesteloo
description: Skyways and Martin UAV completed Navy NAWCAD Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS trials at Naval Air Station Patuxent River, demonstrating cargo delivery over 200 nautical miles to a moving ship.

title: Naval Air Warfare Center Teams up with Military Sealift Command, U.S. Marines to Test UAS Concept
description: Skyways participated in Fleet Battle Problem 23-1 off the coast of North Carolina with NAWCAD, UX-24, USMC, and MSC. The team completed five test flights with two drones demonstrating autonomous cargo delivery ship-to-shore and shore-to-ship.

title: US Naval Components Test Skyways-Developed Blue Water Logistics UAS
description: Skyways' Blue Water Logistics UAS was tested off North Carolina with NAWCAD, UX-24, USMC, and Military Sealift Command during Fleet Battle Problem 23-1.

title: Skyways V3
description: Skyways' V3 aircraft specs and capabilities were publicly profiled for the first time, including company founding story and cargo drone use cases.

title: Navy tests using drones for medical supply deliveries during RIMPAC
author: Diana Correll
description: Skyways' V2.6 was one of two UAS tested aboard the destroyer USS Curtis Wilbur during RIMPAC 2024. The Navy launched and recovered six drones from June 19-24 for the Just In Time Delivery logistics effort.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Skyways received $37M from USAF AFWERX STRATFI to transition the V3 from prototype to full-rate production. The V3 targets 157 nautical miles range with 100 lbs payload, or approximately 35 lbs at 1,000 nautical miles. Described as one of the largest STRATFI contracts ever awarded.

title: Skyways Lands $37M USAF Contract to Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
author: Miriam McNabb
description: Skyways secured a $37M AFWERX contract to scale V3 autonomous cargo drone production. The company self-funds aircraft development and flies full-scale aircraft before seeking contracts.

title: Skyways Lands $37M USAF Deal to Mass-Produce Cargo Drone
author: Joe Saballa
description: Skyways received a $37M USAF AFWERX deal to mass-produce the V3 cargo drone for defense logistics operations.

title: Skyways Wins $37M USAF Contract to Scale V3 Autonomous Cargo Drones in 2025
description: Skyways won a $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract to scale V3 autonomous cargo drone production in 2025.

title: U.S. government to spend millions to buy massive cargo drones capable of flying 1,000 miles
author: Sally French
description: Skyways' V3 autonomous cargo drone received $37M in AFWERX funding. The V3 targets 157 nautical miles range with 100 lbs payload, or approximately 35 lbs at 1,000 nautical miles. AFWERX has awarded over 10,000 contracts totaling $7B+ since 2019.

title: Skyways secures $37M Air Force backing for autonomous cargo planes
description: Skyways secured $37M in Air Force backing through AFWERX STRATFI to scale autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Texas Startup Wins $37M Award to Mass-Produce V3 Cargo Drone
description: Skyways won a $37M AFWERX award to mass-produce the V3 cargo drone for military logistics.

title: Skyways Gets $37 Million to Develop Next-Gen Military Drones
author: Scott Rose
description: Skyways received $37M for V3 development. The company was founded by Charles Acknin, Anurag Sabbarwal, and Chris Craighill. The V2 completed 11 consecutive flights in one day totaling 9.4 hours and 466 nautical miles with 8-minute turnarounds.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $37M USAF AFWERX STRATFI contract for V3 production scale-up.

title: Austin defense tech startup wins contract
description: Skyways, an Austin-based defense tech startup, secured a $37M AFWERX contract for autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Skyways Secures $37M USAF Autonomous Cargo Drone Contract
description: Skyways secured a $37M USAF AFWERX contract for V3 autonomous cargo drone production and scale-up.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract
description: Skyways secured a $37M USAF AFWERX contract for V3 autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Texas Startup Wins $37M Award to Mass-Produce V3 Cargo Drone
description: Skyways won a $37M AFWERX award to transition the V3 autonomous cargo drone from prototype to mass production.
link_notes: Article removed from fullavantenews.com (now FAN Transport Insights). No Wayback Machine archive found. No cached version indexed by search engines. Article was a syndicated version of the same story published on CargoClear.

title: Texas Startup Wins $37M Award to Mass-Produce V3 Cargo Drone
description: Skyways won a $37M AFWERX award to transition the V3 autonomous cargo drone from prototype to mass production.

title: Skyways Get $37M US Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Skyways received a $37M US Air Force AFWERX contract to scale autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Skyways secures US Air Force contract to scale cargo drones
description: Skyways secured a US Air Force AFWERX contract to scale V3 cargo drone production for defense and logistics operations.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract
description: Skyways secured a $37M US Air Force AFWERX contract to advance V3 autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million US Air Force Contract
description: Skyways secured a $37M US Air Force AFWERX contract for V3 autonomous cargo drone production scale-up.

title: Energy Briefs
description: Skyways' $37M AFWERX contract mentioned in an energy industry news roundup.

title: Axios Pro Rata Premium First Look
description: Skyways' $37M AFWERX contract included in Axios Pro Rata's premium deal flow newsletter.

title: Contract News in Brief
description: Skyways' $37M AFWERX contract included in a defense contract news roundup.

title: Skyways Secures $37 Million U.S. Air Force Contract to Rapidly Scale Autonomous Cargo Drones
description: Skyways secured a $37M US Air Force AFWERX contract to scale autonomous cargo drone production.

title: Air Force Hawkeye
description: Skyways' $37M AFWERX contract included in an Air Force technology and acquisition news roundup.

title: Tectonic Newsletter
description: Skyways' $37M AFWERX contract featured in Tectonic's defense technology newsletter.
link_notes: Tectonic Defense is a 5x weekly defense tech newsletter. The Skyways AFWERX mention was in an email edition not publicly archived. Link points to Tectonic news page. Skyways also participated in the Tectonic Defense Summit in Austin with a live field ops demo.

title: Skyways Secures $5 Million Debt Facility to Accelerate Production
description: Skyways secured a $5M credit facility from Leonid Capital Partners for V3 production for the DoD. The company had completed nearly 200 V3 test flights including 8-hour heavy-fuel engine flights at time of announcement.

title: Skyways Secures $5 Million to Boost Production of Autonomous Cargo Drones
author: Miriam McNabb
description: Skyways secured $5M from Leonid Capital Partners to boost V3 autonomous cargo drone production for defense customers.

title: Skyways Secures $5 Million Debt Facility to Accelerate Production
description: Syndicated Skyways press release announcing the $5M Leonid Capital Partners debt facility for V3 production acceleration.

title: Project ULTRA expands drone operations, secures $100M contract ceiling
description: Skyways aircraft are being used for weekly cargo flights between Grand Forks AFB and Cavalier Space Force Station under Project ULTRA. The DoD raised the contract ceiling from $18M to $100M. These were the first drone flights without a chase plane on a military installation.

title: Project ULTRA Secures Contract Boost to $100M and Launches Weekly Cargo Drone Flights
description: Skyways aircraft conducted blood delivery test flights under Project ULTRA as the contract ceiling expanded from $18M to $100M for weekly cargo drone flights between military bases.

title: $100M boost propels North Dakota's Project ULTRA
description: Skyways drones displayed in the GrandSKY hangar as Project ULTRA expanded with a $100M contract ceiling for military drone cargo operations in North Dakota.

title: Sen. Hoeven announces major boost in federal investment in GrandSKY
author: Pamela Knudson
description: Skyways is the aircraft provider for Project ULTRA at GrandSKY, which received expanded federal investment including the state legislature's $33M investment that leveraged $170M in private sector investment.

title: Skyways Completes Historic Cargo Drone Flights Between Airports for U.S. Military
description: Skyways completed 20 operational flights (10 round trips) between Grand Forks AFB and Cavalier Space Force Station, delivering 230 lbs of cargo including temperature-controlled blood. This was the first customer demonstration of V3 Block 1 BVLOS. Skyways was the only UAS provider at the event.

title: Skyways Completes Historic Cargo Drone Flights for U.S. Military
author: Miriam McNabb
description: Skyways completed historic cargo drone flights for the US military under Project ULTRA. Skyways was selected for the program because of its technical maturity and previous success with the Navy and Air Force.

title: Skyways completes historic cargo drone flights in controlled airspace
author: Anastasiya Simsek
description: Skyways completed autonomous cargo drone flights in controlled airspace between two military airports under Project ULTRA.

title: Skyways flies cargo between airports for US military
author: James Muir
description: Skyways flew cargo between Grand Forks AFB and Cavalier Space Force Station for the US military under Project ULTRA.

title: Skyways Conducts Military Cargo UAS Flights Between Airports
description: Skyways conducted military cargo UAS flights between two airports under Project ULTRA. The company was selected for its technical maturity and demonstrated long-range logistics capabilities.

title: GrandSKY's Project ULTRA: Enabling Military BVLOS Ops
description: Skyways' V2.6 and V3.0 aircraft were used for Project ULTRA military BVLOS operations in the National Airspace System at GrandSKY in North Dakota.

title: RWE successfully pioneers cargo drone operations at offshore wind farms
description: Skyways' aircraft completed 80+ successful offshore flights in a multi-phase pilot project at RWE's Arkona offshore wind farm in the Baltic Sea, marking the first repeated BVLOS turbine deliveries in German offshore wind.

title: Skyports Drone Services, RWE, Skyways conduct Germany's first long-range drone deliveries to offshore wind turbines
description: Skyways' V2 flew 40km routes carrying up to 10kg cargo in sub-30-minute flights (vs. 1+ hour by boat) to RWE's Arkona offshore wind turbines. The trial totaled over 2,500km and 65+ flight hours in 3 weeks.

title: Skyways Powers Historic Cargo Deliveries to Offshore Windfarm
description: Skyways powered historic BVLOS cargo deliveries to RWE's Arkona offshore wind farm in the Baltic Sea with partners Skyports Drone Services and RWE.

title: Skyways Autonomous Drone Cuts Offshore Wind Supply Time From 2 Hours To 26 Minutes
author: Haye Kesteloo
description: Skyways' V2 drone flew automated 50-mile round trips carrying up to 22 lbs in 29.7-knot winds to offshore wind turbines, cutting supply time from 2 hours to 26 minutes at the Arkona wind farm.

title: Germany's First Long-Range Drone Deliveries Reach Offshore Wind Turbines
author: Miriam McNabb
description: Skyways' aircraft completed Germany's first long-range BVLOS drone deliveries to offshore wind turbines at RWE's Arkona wind farm in the Baltic Sea.

title: Autonomous Cargo Drone Completes Offshore Windfarm Deliveries
author: Joe Macey
description: Skyways' autonomous cargo drone completed BVLOS deliveries to offshore wind turbines at the Arkona wind farm in the Baltic Sea.

title: Skyports, RWE and Skyways complete Germany's first long-range drone deliveries
description: Skyways, Skyports Drone Services, and RWE completed Germany's first long-range BVLOS drone deliveries to offshore wind turbines at the Arkona wind farm.

title: Skyways Powers Historic Cargo Deliveries to Offshore Windfarm
author: Kim Stevens
description: Skyways powered historic cargo deliveries to RWE's Arkona offshore wind farm using autonomous BVLOS drone operations.

title: RWE pioneers cargo drone operations at offshore wind farms
description: Skyways' aircraft used in RWE's cargo drone operations at the Arkona offshore wind farm in Germany's Baltic Sea.

title: RWE unleashes autonomous heavy-payload drones at German offshore wind farms
description: Skyways' autonomous heavy-payload drones deployed at RWE's Arkona offshore wind farm in the German Baltic Sea.

title: Skyports, RWE, Skyways in long-range offshore drone delivery trial
description: Skyways, Skyports, and RWE conducted a long-range BVLOS offshore drone delivery trial at the Arkona wind farm.

title: RWE tests cargo drones for offshore wind operations in Germany
description: Skyways' aircraft used for RWE's cargo drone testing at the Arkona offshore wind farm in Germany.

title: Drone completes Germany's first offshore delivery trial
description: Skyways' drone completed Germany's first offshore delivery trial at RWE's Arkona wind farm in the Baltic Sea.

title: Skyways: Redefining Autonomous Long-Range Cargo Drones
author: Jacob Stoner
description: Skyways profiled covering the $5M Leonid debt facility, $37M AFWERX contract, V2 and V3 platforms, vertically integrated manufacturing, and Y Combinator Summer 2017 origin.

title: Charles Acknin, Co-Founder & CEO of Skyways (Innovator Series)
author: Jacob Stoner
description: Profile of Skyways CEO Charles Acknin covering his background at Google as a Senior Software Engineer, co-founding Elixir Labs, Oxford Brookes MSc, SUPINFO MSc, and founding Skyways in 2017.

title: Milestone Story: Skyways Delivers Precision Under Pressure with Historic Long-range, Offshore Cargo Drop
description: First-person account of Skyways' Arkona offshore wind trial covering engineering challenges including last-minute tailwind adjustments and the SkyNav ground control system.

title: Skyways: Inside its aspirations to build the world's largest autonomous cargo aircraft fleet
author: Sally French
description: Skyways hosted its inaugural Demo Day at its Giddings, TX test site. ANA Holdings invested $1M through its corporate venture fund. The V3 is technically the seventh-generation Skyways aircraft. The company has been generating revenue since early in its existence.

title: Austin aviation company looking to scale autonomous drones
description: Skyways demonstrated V2 and V3 aircraft at its Demo Day in Giddings, TX. CEO Charles Acknin and test operator Alberto Gomez discussed autonomous cargo delivery for contested environments.

title: From cargo to commuters — Skyways' roadmap to human flight
author: Sally French
description: Skyways CEO Charles Acknin discussed the company's long-term roadmap from cargo to human flight. Covers Starlink adoption for connectivity, supply chain shift away from Chinese components, and the defense-first strategy.

title: Skyways V3 Autonomous Cargo Drone (Paywalled)
description: Skyways' V3 aircraft profiled in Janes behind its paywall, reaching 100,000+ subscribers including industry professionals, members of Congress, and government officials.
link_notes: Confirmed behind Janes paywall (100,000+ subscribers). Not indexed by search engines. Two earlier Skyways/Janes articles are publicly accessible but this January 2026 V3 piece is not. No workaround available without a Janes subscription.

title: ANA to Launch Drone Logistics Nationwide — Delivering Medicine to Remote Islands and Food to Isolated Areas During Disasters
description: Skyways and ANA operations in Okinawa covered in Japan's largest newspaper (5.7M+ morning circulation). Published in the Economy section behind a paywall.
original_title: ＡＮＡがドローン物流を全国で事業化へ…離島に医薬品、災害時は孤立地域へ食料を輸送

title: Skyways Strengthens Leadership Team to Accelerate Rapid Growth
description: Skyways announced Bill Wimberley as CCO, Isaac Roberts transitioning to CSO, and key hires: Gabrielle Wain (VP Regulatory, ex-Zipline), Mike Uffelman (Head of Flight Ops, ex-USAF, 2,500+ flight hours), and Jeff Weinstein (VP Production, 25+ years experience). The company doubled revenue for three consecutive years.

title: Bill Wimberley Joins Skyways as Chief Commercial Officer
description: Bill Wimberley joined Skyways as Chief Commercial Officer as part of the company's leadership expansion.

title: Austin Skyways autonomous drone aircraft
author: Karoline Leonard
description: Skyways profiled in Austin's major newspaper. V2 costs ~$1,000/flight hour vs $30,000 for helicopter. The company has ~30 employees with plans to double in 2026 and operates a 250,000 sq ft facility at Tech Ridge in North Austin with a 750-acre test site near Giddings.

title: Skyways Air Transportation Plans World's Largest Autonomous Drone Fleet With $37M Air Force Backing
author: Haye Kesteloo
description: Skyways plans to build the world's largest autonomous drone fleet backed by the $37M Air Force contract. The FY2026 defense budget includes $9.4B for aerial drones.

title: Skyways Air Transportation Plans World's Largest Autonomous Drone Fleet
description: Skyways plans to build the world's largest autonomous drone fleet backed by the $37M Air Force AFWERX contract.

title: Making Autonomous Cargo Work at Real-World Scale (Drone Radio Show Episode DRS 451)
description: Skyways CEO Charles Acknin discussed how the company is turning autonomous cargo drones into dependable, scalable logistics systems, covering operational status, mass production plans, and the long-term passenger vision.

title: ANA to enter drone delivery business by FY2028 with eye on remote islands
description: ANA Holdings plans to use Skyways' drones for automated delivery by March 2029, targeting remote Japanese islands. The Skyways drone is 3 meters in length with a 7-meter wingspan, carries ~50kg, and has 1,600km max range.

title: ANA HD completes long-distance medical supply drone delivery trials in Okinawa
description: ANA completed drone delivery trials using Skyways aircraft from October 2025 through January 2026 in Okinawa, flying routes of 89-100km. ANA signed a long-term business partnership agreement with Skyways and established internal Skyways-specific pilot training.

title: ANA HD Completes Long-Distance Medical Supply Drone Delivery Trials
description: ANA completed long-distance medical supply drone delivery trials in Okinawa using Skyways aircraft, establishing a pilot training system specific to Skyways' platform.
